seo.title: API integration checklist | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: A practical API integration checklist covering data ownership, error states, exceptions, monitoring, and real operational constraints.

page.title: API integration checklist | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: API integration checklist before delivery starts
page.description: A practical API integration checklist covering data ownership, error states, exceptions, monitoring, and real operational constraints.

hero.title: Integration projects usually fail on the details nobody named early enough
hero.subtitle: The checklist helps teams work through data flow, exceptions, responsibility, and operational reality before the integration becomes fragile.

intro: API integrations look simpler than they are. The real trouble often lives in exceptions, data quality, and unclear ownership.
A practical checklist reduces the risk of turning the integration into another source of manual work and hidden failure.

sections.title: What the template should cover
body: The point is not paperwork for its own sake. The value is clearer decision-making and fewer project blind spots.

sections.title: How to use it in practice
body: The template should work as a practical aid before an introductory call, scoping session, or takeover phase.
bullets: as input to prioritising integration work

sections.title: What outcome it should create
body: A good template shortens the path to the first useful project decision.

faq.question: Is the checklist useful without an in-house technical team?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps structure the important questions for your supplier or internal IT partner.

faq.question: Does the checklist replace architecture work?
faq.answer: No. It is an input tool that helps the architecture work start from a better position.

faq.question: Can it be used for internal workflow integrations too?
faq.answer: Yes. That is one of the most practical uses for it.

notFor: collecting detail with no decision attached
